english: = yčig (gcig) one, as the first part of compound numbers: čig-bču (cig bcu) 10, čig-brgya (cig brgya) 100, čig-stoṅ (cig stong) 1000, čig-kri (cig khri) a myriad etc.; also: čig-rkyaṅ (cig rkyang) Lex., Schr.: 'separate, single, one alone'; čig-skyés (cig skyes) Med., čig-táṅ (cig tang) Med.? — čig-túb (cig thub) n. of a plant Med.; Sch. also: čig-túb-pa (cig thub pa) to be able to do a thing alone; čig-dril (cig dril) Sch.: rolled, wrapped, packed up (in one parcel or bundle); čig-láb byéd-pa (cig lab byed pa) to talk to one's self, to hold a soliloquy Schr.

resp. horse, riding-horse, saddle-horse, čibs-la _očib-pa (chibs la 'chib pa) (for rtá-la zón-pa (rta la zhon pa)) to get on horseback, to mount; to go on horseback, to ride; čibs-las yzól-ba (chibs las gzhol ba) to dismount, *čibs zól-la naṅ* (chibs zhol la nang) C. may your honour please to dismount; kyéd-kyi čibs-su _obul (khyed kyi chibs su 'bul) I give it you for a riding-horse Mil. Comp. čibs-ka khríd-pa (chibs kha khrid pa) to lead a horse by the bridle Schr.; čibs-ka túb-pa (chibs kha thub pa) to have the command of the bridle, fig.: to be expert in ruling Ld.-Glr. p. 14, a, Schl. where pyibs (phyibs) is incorr.). — čibs-čás (chibs chas) a horse's furniture, harness Cs. — čibs-túr (chibs thur) the head-piece of a bridle. — čibs-dpón (chibs dpon) a master groom, equerry. — čibs-rá (chibs ra) a stable for horses.

I. num. fig.: 66. II. sbst. (resp. čab (chab)) 1. water; ču daṅ sai bu (chu dang sa'i bu) is said to be a poetical name for wood; _obáb-ču ('bab chu) lit. descending water, viz. brook, river; also rain. — 2. brook, river, ču _okyám-po (chu 'khyam po) overflowing rivers, floods Ma.; táṅ-ču (thang chu) a river or rivulet of the plain; ri-yzár-ču (ri gzar chu) cataract, mountain torrent Glr. — 3. water in the body: sñiṅ-ču (snying chu) dropsy in the pericardium, págs-ču (pags chu) anasarca Med.; págs-ču-zugs (pags chu zugs) one suffering from anasarca; v. also ču-sér (chu ser); esp. euphem for urine; mí-ču (mi chu) urine of men, bá-ču (ba chu) of cows Med.: ču ni ču _odra (chu ni chu 'dra) the urine is like water Med. — 4. v. ču-żéṅ (chu zheng). Comp. ču-klúṅ (chu klung) river, e.g. ču-kluṅ gaṅ-gá (chu klung gang ga) the river Ganges Dzl. — ču-klóṅ (chu klong) Cs.: 'the body of a river', yet v. klóṅ (klong). — ču-dkyil (chu dkyil) the middle of a river. — ču-rkyál (chu rkyal) a leather bag for water Cs. — ču-skád (chu skad) the voice of the waters, the sound of rushing water. — ču-skór, raṅ-tág-ču-skór (chu skor, rang thag chu skor) water-mill Glr. — ču-skyúr (chu skyur) n. of a bird Thgy., Sch.: 'bittern, snipe'; also n. of a plant. — ču-skyúr (chu skyur) 1. Lt.: acidulous mineral waters 2. C.: vinegar. — ču-skyés (chu skyes) 'water-born', the lotus Glr. — ču-skyór (chu skyor) a handful of water. ču-ka (chu ka) the bank or brink of a river. — ču-kúg (chu khug) bay, gulf. — ču-_okúr (chu 'khur) containing water, po. for cloud; a native proposed to use this word also for sponge, which is a commodity hitherto unknown in Tibet. — ču-_okyil (chu 'khyil) puddle, pool. — ču-gáṅ (chu gang) 1. full of water. 2. = ču-sgáṅ (chu sgang) (v. sgáṅ (sgang)) which latter is prob. the more correct spelling. 3. Dzl. 118, 2; 222, 18 = सत्त्व virtue, honesty, v. Schf. on this passage. — ču-grí (chu gri) a sort of knife; Tar. 43, 1 Schf. razor; also the attribute of a god, a weapon with a curved blade Stg. — ču-gróg (chu grog) Sch.: rivulet, brook; dish - water, rinsings; boiled water (?). — ču-mgó (chu mgo) C. source or head of a river. — ču-gágs (chu gags) stoppage or retention of urine, ischury, ču-gágs _obigs (chu gags 'bigs) the ischury is removed (lit. bored through) Med. — ču-grám (chu gram) bank of a river; ču-grám-gyi śiṅ (chu gram gyi shing) a tree on the edge of a river, a metaphor for frail and perishable things. — ču-rgyún (chu rgyun) the streaming, continual flowing, current, often fig. — ču-sgóṅ (chu sgong) the water-egg, po. for moon Sch. — ču-ṅógs (chu ngogs) v. ṅógs (ngogs). — *ču-ta-gir* (chu ta gir) W. flour-dumplings, boiled in water. — *ču-stán* (chu stan) W. swaddling-cloth. — *ču-tág* (chu thag) W. calamus, sweet-scented flag, or some similar plant. — ču-túms (chu thums) Sch.: 'a swelling in the flesh, or a tumour filled with water'. — ču-mtá (chu mtha') the side or bank of a river, *ču-tá tsúg-pa* (chu ta tshug pa) (the avalanche came down) even to the river side. — ču-dár (chu dar) Wdk. a small prayer-flag stuck up close to the river, in order to avert inundations. — ču-dúg (chu dug) Sch.: 'a poisonous plant, hemlock', but Tibetans usually understand by it the stupefying power ascribed to certain rivers. — ču-doṅ (chu dong) a deep well. —
